texto: Now I have two immediate objects in view. The first is to devote myself to the evolutionary life more thoroughly than I have yet done—to think, speak, do nothing but what is evolutionary. Hitherto I have been little more than a passive Evolutionist. Henceforth I shall be the active agent, the apostle of Evolution. I shall give Evolution ample opportunity to vindicate my fitness, and that as publicly as possible in order to convert others.
explicacao: Citação de John Davidson sobre sentido da existência e experiência vivida. “Now I have two immediate objects in view. The first is to devote myself to the evolutionary life …” articula reflexao, evolution, evolutionary em registro de reflexao, com relevância para contextos como academia e reflexao.

texto: My approach is so simple; every song I sing, every story I tell, every move I make, must move the audience to laughter, tears, or inspiration. Otherwise, why do it? It's the communication.
explicacao: Pergunta retórica de John Davidson que provoca temas ligados a motivacao. Ao questionar “My approach is so simple; every song I sing, every story I tell, every move I make, must move the…”, o texto convida o leitor a revisar crenças sobre motivacao, laughter, simple, com foco em inspiracao e simple.

texto: Do I believe in Heaven and Hell? I do, we have them here; the world is nothing else.
explicacao: Pergunta retórica de John Davidson que provoca temas ligados a reflexao. Ao questionar “Do I believe in Heaven and Hell? I do, we have them here; the world is nothing else.”, o texto convida o leitor a revisar crenças sobre reflexao, heaven, hell, com foco em hell.

texto: You’re young, and like anything new. It’s change you want. I’m middle-aged, and there’s nothing staler to me than change. Constant comfort and little luxuries as regular as the clock are fresher than change.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a John Davidson: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de temas ligados a reflexao. O trecho “You’re young, and like anything new. It’s change you want. I’m middle-aged, and there’s nothing s…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que reflexao, change, middle-age pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de mudanca e middle-age.

texto: Seek for no meaning in it; it has none. What meaning is there in pain and pleasure? They are twins; that is all we know. Seek no meaning in anything you see here. Images, ideas, flashes of purpose will peer out in all our ways and deeds, but there is no intention here below. Is there any intention anywhere?
explicacao: Pergunta retórica de John Davidson que provoca esforço, persistência e resultados. Ao questionar “Seek for no meaning in it; it has none. What meaning is there in pain and pleasure? They are twin…”, o texto convida o leitor a revisar crenças sobre reflexao, vida, pain, com foco em pain e pleasure.
